section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
claim: The nominal composition of the studied steel is Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu.
evidence_text: The Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-(0,3)Cu austenitic low-density steel (0Cu steel or 3Cu steel) with nominal composition

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-(0,3)Cu steel
claim: The steel was processed by solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 hour and aging treatment at 550°C for 3 hours.
evidence_text: processed by solution treatment (at 1000℃ for 1 h) and aging treatment (at 550℃ for 3 h) sequentially

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The Cu-rich phase precipitates directly in FCC structure in the austenitic matrix.
evidence_text: the Cu-rich phase in austenitic precipitates directly in the FCC structure

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The Cu-rich phase has a BCC structure according to SAED analysis.
evidence_text: the Cu-rich phase has BCC structure (Fig. 1, inset)

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval with a diameter of ~10 nm and a small amount of bar shapes with a length of ~30 nm.
evidence_text: The Cu-rich phase is mainly irregular oval and has a small amount of bars shapes, which the diameter of irregular oval Cu-rich phase is ~ 10 nm and the length of bars shapes Cu-rich phase is ~ 30 nm

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The Cu-rich phase crystal plane spacing (002) is 0.182 nm, consistent with FCC Cu.
evidence_text: Cu-rich phase crystal plane spacing (00 2) is 0.182 nm which is consistent with Cu in FCC structure

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The lattice parameter of FCC Cu-rich phase is a = 0.3652 nm and of κ-carbide is a = 0.3775 nm.
evidence_text: The lattice parameter of FCC Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide are a = 0.3652 nm and a = 0.3775 nm

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: austenitic matrix in solution-treated 3Cu steel
claim: The lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix under solution treatment is a = 0.3689 nm obtained by XRD.
evidence_text: The lattice parameter of austenitic matrix under solution treatment is a = 0.3689 nm obtained by XRD

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: lattice misfit between phases in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The lattice misfit between κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase is 3.4%, between κ-carbide and austenitic matrix is 2.3%, and between Cu-rich phase and austenitic matrix is 1.0%.
evidence_text: the lattice misfit between κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase is 3.4 %, the lattice misfit between κ-carbide and austenitic matrix is 2.3 %, and the lattice misfit between Cu-rich phase and austenitic matrix is 1.0 %

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide
claim: Due to lattice misfit between the three phases being less than 5%, they are in a coherent relationship.
evidence_text: Due to the lattice misfit between the three phases being less than 5 %, the three phases are in a coherent relationship

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: Cu-rich phase precipitation
claim: Cu-rich phase has a lower nucleation energy barrier in austenitic matrix because its lattice misfit with austenitic matrix is lower than that with κ-carbide.
evidence_text: Cu-rich phase has a lower nucleation energy barrier in austenitic matrix. Cu-rich phase precipitated in austenitic matrix instead of κ-carbide

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase co-precipitation
claim: κ-carbide precipitates first, and its precipitation promotes Cu-rich phase precipitation by increasing Cu concentration near κ-carbide.
evidence_text: κ-carbide precipitated from austenitic matrix, and Cu atoms migrate to the austenitic matrix without κ-carbide, thereby increasing the Cu concentration near the κ-carbide. As the κ-carbide further precipitate, the Cu concentration around κ-carbide increases

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: aged 3Cu steel
claim: TEM-EDS mapping shows that κ-carbide (white dashed box) excludes Cu atoms, while Cu-rich phase (magenta line area) excludes Fe, Mn, and Al atoms.
evidence_text: TEM-EDS mapping shows that Fe, Mn and Al atoms exhibited brighter contrast within the white dashed box zone, while Cu atoms exhibit darker contrast. However, Cu atoms exhibit brighter contrast within the magenta line area

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: κ-carbide precipitation in Fe-Mn-Al-C steel
claim: The critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix for κ-carbide precipitation is about a = 0.3670 nm; below this, κ-carbide cannot precipitate intragranularly.
evidence_text: The critical lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix is about a = 0.3670 nm, which is lower than the critical lattice parameter, and the κ-carbide cannot precipitate in the austenitic matrix

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: effect of Cu on lattice parameter
claim: Adding Cu to 0Cu steel increases the lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix from 0.3687 nm to 0.3689 nm, which is beneficial for κ-carbide precipitation.
evidence_text: the lattice parameter of 0Cu steel and 3Cu steel under solid solution treatment are a = 0.3687 nm and a = 0.3689 nm, respectively. Moreover, adding Cu element to 0Cu steel can increase the lattice parameter of the austenitic matrix, which is beneficial for the precipitation of κ-carbide

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: critical composition for intragranular κ-carbide in Fe-Mn-Al-C steel
claim: A composition boundary formula for intragranular κ-carbide precipitation is: 0.208(wt.%C) + 0.098(wt.%Al) = 1 - 0.0054(wt.%Mn).
evidence_text: a composition boundary formula for the precipitation of intragranular κ-carbides in Fe-Mn-Al-C steel is as follows: 0.208(wt.% C) + 0.098(wt.% Al) = 1 - 0.0054(wt.% Mn)

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: critical Al content for κ-carbide in 0Cu steel
claim: The critical Al content for κ-carbide precipitation in 0Cu steel is 6.52 wt%, and the Al content in 0Cu steel exceeds this threshold.
evidence_text: the critical Al content for κ-carbide precipitation in 0Cu steel is 6.52 wt%. However, the Al content in 0Cu steel exceeds 6.52 wt%

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: interfacial energy for κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase
claim: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix is 0.025 J/m^2 and of Cu-rich phase with austenitic matrix is 0.086 J/m^2, both below 0.1 J/m^2.
evidence_text: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-Mn-Al-C steel is 0.025 J/m^2 ... The interfacial energy of Cu in austenitic matrix is 0.086 J/m^2

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: orientation relationship in aged 3Cu steel
claim: The κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have a cube-on-cube orientation relationship with austenitic matrix: [110]γ//[110]κ//[110]Cu and (111)γ//(111)κ//(111)Cu, as shown in Fig. 1.
evidence_text: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have a cube-on-cube orientation relationship with the austenitic matrix: [11 0] //[11 0] //[110] and (11 1) //(11 1) //(11 1)

section: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
subject: interfacial energy of κ-carbide vs Cu-rich phase
claim: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix (0.025 J/m^2) is lower than that of Cu-rich phase (0.086 J/m^2), explaining earlier precipitation of κ-carbide during aging.
evidence_text: The interfacial energy of κ-carbide with austenitic matrix is lower than Cu-rich phase with austenitic matrix. Therefore, in the early stage of aging treatment, κ-carbide precipitate earlier than Cu-rich phases
